item.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: The suit poses a new challenge to the $110 billion deal that would unite two of the nation's largest media companies.

item.title: ICE halts most vehicle stops following deadly shootings, sources say
item.description: Neither of the victims of the ICE shootings in Maine or Texas were the target of enforcement operations, according to the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: U.S. blockade on Iran restarts as Trump angles for control over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S. restarts its Iran blockade and carries out more strikes, President Trump has called off a planned 20% fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Maine was not intended target of warrant, lawmakers say
item.description: Maine Sen. Angus King said he told Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin that he wanted a transparent investigation into the shooting in Biddeford.

item.title: N.Y. enacts nation's first statewide moratorium on building new data centers
item.description: Building new data centers in New York will be paused for a year in order to allow state officials to establish guidelines protecting residents and the environment, Gov. Kathy Hochul said.

item.title: Senate Democrats block must-pass defense policy bill over Iran war objections
item.description: Senate Democrats blocked a must-pass annual defense policy bill from moving forward on Tuesday as they voiced opposition to the Trump administration's handling of the war with Iran.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Supreme Court pushes Congress for more security: "Threats have come very close"
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett urged Congress to provide additional funding to enhance protection for the justices as they face a rise in threats.

item.title: Bipartisan senators introduce Russia sanctions and tariff bill Graham championed
item.description: The text of the bill had been finalized before Graham met with Zelenskyy last week in Kyiv.

item.title: House GOP leaders break logjam after conservative revolt
item.description: The House on Tuesday finally broke an impasse that had stalled most legislative action on the floor.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, sworn in as senator
item.description: Darline Graham, the sister of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, was sworn in to serve out the remainder of his term in the Senate, following his sudden death over the weekend.

item.title: DoD watchdog finds 155mm artillery plant built 2 years ago has produced nothing
item.description: An ammunition plant in Mesquite, Texas, has not produced any metal projectile parts after the Army spent $469 million to establish the facility.

item.title: Darline Graham sworn in as U.S. senator, replacing her late brother Lindsey
item.description: Darline Graham was sworn into the U.S. Senate on Tuesday, replacing her late brother Lindsey, who died unexpectedly over the weekend. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh discusses central bank's independence, inflation in House testimony
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh testified before the House Financial Services Committee Tuesday as new government data showed inflation rose 3.5% annually in June. Taurean Small reports from Washington, D.C.

item.title: Blanche set to face harsh questioning at Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche will appear Wednesday before the Senate Judiciary Committee for his confirmation hearing to take over the role on a permanent basis.

item.title: Trump reverses course on Strait of Hormuz tolls, says U.S. military doesn't need to be in Iran
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday reversed course on his decision to impose a 20% toll on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and said the U.S. military doesn't need to have a presence in Iran. CBS News' senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe and Middle East contributor Courtney Kealy report.

item.title: Insider at "The Wolf of Wall Street" firm opens up: "My lifestyle was insane"
item.description: The Paramount+ documentary "The Real Wolf of Wall Street" gives an inside look at Jordan Belfort's scandal-ridden firm, Stratton Oakmont.

item.title: "Miracle on the Hudson" pilot, Capt. "Sully," shares Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Capt. Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, who famously landed a passenger jet on the Hudson River in 2009, said he has been diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease.

item.title: U.S. marine biologist shot dead in the Philippines, companion injured
item.description: Kent Carpenter and his Filipina companion were at his house in the coastal town of Sibulan when masked men forced their way in.

item.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5M from Trump in abuse, defamation case
item.description: A jury concluded in 2023 that Trump should pay Carroll $5 million in damages.

item.title: Hochul halts New York data center construction amid energy, cost and environmental concerns
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul imposed a year-long moratorium on "hyperscale" AI data centers, halting construction as the state investigates the wider economic and environmental impacts. Shanelle Kaul explains more.

item.title: Social Security's 2027 COLA could be the biggest since 2023
item.description: New estimates call for a 3.6% to 3.8% benefit increase next year, although the final adjustment will depend on inflation readings released this summer.

item.title: Inflation eased more than expected in June, CPI report shows
item.description: Lower gasoline prices slowed inflation in June, though many household costs remained stubbornly high.

item.title: Trump says countries called and protested his 20% Strait of Hormuz fee, reverses course
item.description: President Trump says he changed his mind on a 20% fee he planned to impose on all cargo traversing the Strait of Hormuz. Mr. Trump commented on tensions with Iran as he met with Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi at the White House. CBS News' Aaron Navarro and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
item.description: Investigators say her Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

item.title: Joan Sebastian Guerrero's ICE shooting sparks demands for investigation
item.description: Joan Sebastian Guerrero's death in Maine marks the second time in a week that Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have shot and killed a civilian. The Department of Homeland Security alleged that a man "attempted to flee the scene" when stopped by ICE. Guerrero's killing has prompted calls for an investigation. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: U.S. and Iran continue attacks as Trump vows to control Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have traded strikes for the third night in a row and the U.S. used an explosive sea drone for the first time to attack an Iranian port. Meanwhile, President Trump has claimed the U.S. will control the Strait of Hormuz and impose a 20% fee on cargo. Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Why gaining full control of Strait of Hormuz has proven so hard for U.S.
item.description: Restoring oil tanker traffic in the vital Middle East shipping corridor to prewar levels likely will require a much bigger armada of U.S. warships if not tens of thousands of American troops on Iranian soil, experts say.

item.title: Searching for Maya Millete
item.description: A young mother disappears. Investigators say her husband contacted spellcasters to put a hex on his wife so she wouldn't leave him. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports. | Case update: On July 9, 2026, a jury found Larry Millete guilty of first-degree murder in the death of Maya Millete.

item.title: Timeline: The disappearance of Maya Millete
item.description: Maya Millete, 39, went missing in January 2021. What was happening between her and her husband before she disappeared and what does spellcasting have to do with it?
